tasks.title: Add human review gate to change-orchestrator prompt

subtasks.title: Insert review-wait rule between tasks completion and change-implementor launch
subtasks.scenario: Scenario: Complete artifacts trigger review wait; Scenario: Implementor is not launched before review

subtasks.title: Require explicit human confirmation and name PRD/design/specs/tasks in the review message
subtasks.scenario: Scenario: Human confirms continuation; Scenario: Ambiguous response does not approve; Scenario: Review prompt is actionable

subtasks.title: Encode prompt-only resume semantics: re-prompt safely on session gap when no durable marker exists
Encode artifact-change invalidation: re-open gate if PRD, design, specs, or tasks change after review
subtasks.scenario: Scenario: Tasks change after review request; Scenario: Design changes after approval; Scenario: Approved artifacts stay stable

subtasks.title: Carve out parent large-change decomposition so it is not gated as implementation
subtasks.scenario: Scenario: Parent split continues planning flow

subtasks.title: Preserve earlier-blockers precedence so missing PRD/design/specs/tasks block before the review gate
subtasks.scenario: Scenario: Missing tasks block before review

tasks.title: Document human review gate in change-orchestrator design docs

subtasks.title: Document routing order: artifact prerequisite checks, then human review gate, then change-implementor
Document resume semantics: prompt-only re-prompts safely on resume; durable marker reserved for future need
Document schema restraint: no new status token or schema field by default
Document parent large-change decomposition nuance so planning flow is not gated as implementation
subtasks.scenario: Scenario: Parent Change flow is maintained

tasks.title: Add render contract tests for change-orchestrator gate wording

subtasks.title: Test rendered orchestrator body contains the human review gate wording
Test the gate wording appears before change-implementor and requires explicit human confirmation
Test that removing the gate wording causes at least one render test to fail
Test metadata parity: rendered metadata matches updated source and a body-only gate does not require unrelated metadata changes
subtasks.scenario: Scenario: Body-only gate; Scenario: Metadata changes with prompt semantics
